User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user in the Codebase Collab application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the user entity.

CodeDocument.title: Code Document
CodeDocument.description: A single document containing a user's code snippet.

createdAt.description: Timestamp of when the document was created.

definition.description: Stores user profiles.  `userId` is the Firebase Auth UID.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Auth UID of the user.

definition.description: Stores codebases owned by a specific user. `userId` is the Firebase Auth UID. Includes version history within each document.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Auth UID of the owner user.

definition.description: Stores codebases shared among multiple users. Includes denormalized 'members' map for authorization independence and allows secure listing. 'members' map: {uid: role}.

params.name: codebaseId
params.description: Unique ID for the collaborative codebase.

definition.description: Stores revision history of shared codebases. `codebaseId` references the collaborative codebase. Includes denormalized 'members' map copied from parent /codebases/{codebaseId} for authorization independence.

params.name: codebaseId
params.description: Unique ID for the collaborative codebase.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Auth UID of the owner user.

params.name: documentId
params.description: Unique ID for the code document.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to support the Codebase Collab application, emphasizing secure code storage, collaborative editing, and version control. The structure prioritizes Authorization Independence by denormalizing authorization data (specifically, members and roles) into subcollections, eliminating the need for `get()` calls in security rules and ensuring atomic operations. Structural Segregation is used to isolate code files based on ownership and collaboration needs. Access Modeling employs path-based ownership for private user data and Membership Maps for collaborative projects. QAPs are achieved through collection segregation and denormalized membership maps. The structure also aims for Data Clarity and Predictability through explicit state modeling and standardized naming conventions.

The `/users/{userId}` collection stores user profiles. The `/users/{userId}/codebases/{codebaseId}` collection stores codebases owned by individual users. The `/codebases/{codebaseId}` collection stores codebases shared among multiple collaborators, with the 'members' map denormalized to control access. Version control is implemented within each codebase document, creating immutable revisions.
